key_functions: Define standards for architects and data engineers

accountable_for: Business Ontology document ownership and maintenance
key_responsibilities: Define and maintain business ontology standards

properties.rule_statement: Adopt FIBO as a core standard for business ontology development
Adopt ISO 20022 as a core standard for business ontology development

key_functions: Publish and govern business ontology standards

properties.definition: Defines the core concepts, entities, relationships, and constraints that govern FirstUK Bank's enterprise; provides a shared semantic understanding of business domains to enable knowledge representation, data integration, and enterprise reasoning.
business_rules: Establishes governance constraints for the enterprise

properties.what_it_measures: Total number of business entities defined within the ontology
Total number of mapped relationships within the ontology
Total number of governance constraints defined in the ontology

properties.rule_statement: Concepts map to real-world business entities
Concepts maintain consistent definitions across documents
Critical business concepts are defined
Definitions are unambiguous and precise
Concepts link to policies, procedures, and systems

properties.definition: People and organizations interacting with the bank
Banking products and services
Customer accounts and holdings
Money movement and account activity
Regulatory and risk concepts
Organizational structure and roles
Any natural person or organization that interacts with FirstUK Bank.
A natural person (individual) who holds at least one account with FirstUK Bank.
business_rules: Every customer must have a valid identity
Every customer must pass AML screening before account opening
Customer can hold multiple accounts
Customer can have joint account holders

properties.rule_statement: Every customer must have a valid identity.
Every customer must pass AML screening before account opening.
Customer can hold multiple accounts.
Customer can have joint account holders.

properties.definition: Physical location associated with a customer, account, or branch.
business_rules: UK customers must have a UK address
Address must be verified (utility bill, council tax, etc.)
Addresses use UK postcode format

properties.rule_statement: UK customers must have a UK address
Address must be verified (utility bill, council tax, etc.)
Addresses use UK postcode format (A(1,2)9(1,2)A 9A(2))

properties.definition: Official document used to verify customer identity (KYC)
business_rules: Verification status must be tracked (Unverified, Verified, Expired, Rejected)
Expired documents are rejected

properties.definition: A financial service offered by FirstUK Bank
A savings product offered by FirstUK Bank
A specific capability or service included in a product.
A specific rate of interest applied to accounts.

business_rules.rule_statement: Rate reviews are linked to Bank of England base rate plus a margin.
business_rules.applies_to: Interest Rate calculation and review process

properties.definition: A contractual arrangement between a customer and FirstUK Bank to hold money, make transactions, and earn interest.
business_rules: Every account must have one primary account holder
Account balance is calculated from transaction ledger
Account can only be in one status at a time
Closed accounts are not reactivated

properties.definition: Party entering a contractual arrangement with FirstUK Bank to hold money and make transactions.

relationships_to: Account (1:many, used to calculate account balance)
key_attributes: Contributes to transaction ledger for balance calculation

relationships_to: Account (1:many, includes primary and joint holders)

properties.context: Earned by accounts per contractual arrangement with FirstUK Bank

properties.rule_statement: Every account must have one primary account holder (customer)
Account balance is calculated from transaction ledger
Account can only be in one status at a time
Closed accounts are not reactivated

properties.definition: A person with legal rights and responsibilities for an account.
business_rules: Account must have at least one primary holder
Joint account: Both holders must pass KYC/AML
Either joint holder can withdraw full balance
Interest calculated on total balance (not per holder)
properties.example: Joint Account ACC-00002 with Primary Holder John Smith and Joint Holder Jane Smith

properties.definition: Mandatory compliance verification process for account holders.
business_rules: Both joint account holders must pass KYC/AML screening
properties.regulatory_significance: Required to mitigate financial crime risk on joint accounts

properties.definition: Financial container linked to one or more account holders and customers.
business_rules: Interest calculated on total balance (not per holder)

properties.rule_statement: Account must have at least one primary holder
Both joint account holders must pass KYC/AML screening
Either joint holder can withdraw the full account balance
properties.consequence: Both holders remain liable for any overdraft

properties.rule_statement: Interest is calculated on the total account balance, not allocated per holder

properties.definition: An account held by two or more customers with joint and several liability.
business_rules: Both holders must consent to account closure
Both holders responsible for overdraft
Both holders can initiate transactions
Dispute resolution per Complaint Handling
Both holders jointly and severally liable
Both included in AML due diligence
Both entitled to account information
properties.regulatory_significance: Joint and several liability applies; mandatory AML due diligence for all holders

properties.rule_statement: Both holders must consent to account closure
Both holders responsible for overdraft
Both holders can initiate transactions
Dispute resolution per Complaint Handling department/process
Both holders are jointly and severally liable for the account
Both account holders must be included in AML due diligence

properties.definition: A payment card (debit, credit, or prepaid) linked to an account.
business_rules: Card can be locked/unlocked by cardholder
Lost/stolen cards can be replaced (fee £15)
Only one card per account (initially)
Contactless limit: £100 per transaction

properties.rule_statement: Card can be locked/unlocked by cardholder
Lost/stolen cards can be replaced (fee £15)
Only one card per account (initially)
Contactless limit: £100 per transaction

properties.definition: A financial event representing money movement or activity on an account.
business_rules: Transaction cannot be deleted (audit trail)
Account balance = sum of all transactions
Each transaction linked to fraud risk score
All transactions logged for compliance

relationships_to: linked to transactions via fraud detection (1:many)

business_rules: Classified as a recurring debit transaction
Classified as a bill payment debit transaction

properties.rule_statement: Transaction cannot be deleted
Account balance = sum of all transactions
Each transaction linked to fraud risk score
All transactions logged for compliance

properties.definition: A recurring transfer initiated by the account holder.
business_rules: Creates automatic transaction each period
Amount and frequency cannot change (must cancel and create new)
Survives account holder changes if joint
Requires 30-day notice for cancellation except in fraud/closure cases
Changes in account holder do not invalidate standing order if joint

properties.rule_statement: Amount and frequency cannot change; must cancel and create a new standing order to modify.
properties.exception_process: Cancel existing order and create a new one

properties.rule_statement: Requires 30-day notice prior to cancellation.
properties.exception_process: Exemptions apply for fraud or account closure

properties.rule_statement: Standing orders survive account holder changes if the account is joint.

properties.definition: A recurring payment initiated by a third party (biller) authorized by the customer.
business_rules: Customer authorizes specific biller via DD mandate
Biller cannot debit more than maximum amount without notice
Customer can cancel DD up to 5 days before payment
Failed DD can be retried (usually once)
DD protected by Direct Debit Guarantee

properties.definition: Authorizes third-party recurring payments and holds cancellation rights
business_rules: Can cancel DD up to 5 days before payment

properties.definition: Entity holding funds for biller payments
Record generated by Direct Debit processing

properties.rule_statement: Direct Debit payments are protected under the Direct Debit Guarantee scheme
properties.consequence: Provides protection against unauthorized or incorrect debits

properties.rule_statement: Customer must authorize a specific biller via a Direct Debit mandate before payment initiation
Biller cannot debit more than the specified maximum amount without prior notice
Customer can cancel Direct Debit up to 5 days before the scheduled payment date
Failed Direct Debit payments can be retried, typically limited to one attempt

properties.definition: Know Your Customer verification confirming customer identity and legitimacy.
business_rules: KYC mandatory before account opening
KYC refreshed periodically (every 3–5 years)
High-risk customers require Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD)
KYC documents retained for 6 years post-closure

properties.rule_statement: KYC verification must be completed before an account can be opened.
KYC verification must be refreshed every 3 to 5 years.
Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) is required for high-risk customers.
KYC documents must be retained for 6 years after account closure.

properties.definition: Anti-Money Laundering screening to detect financial crime risks
business_rules: AML screening mandatory at account opening
Ongoing AML monitoring for high-risk customers
Hit on sanctions list triggers automatic account freeze and SAR filing
Failed screening results in account application rejection

properties.definition: US Treasury sanctions list used for AML screening
properties.regulatory_significance: Mandatory US and international financial crime compliance

properties.definition: United Nations sanctions list used for AML screening
European Union sanctions list used for AML screening
UK HM Treasury sanctions designations used for AML screening
International police notices for fugitives used in AML screening
Databases tracking Politically Exposed Persons for risk assessment
properties.regulatory_significance: High-risk customer identification and enhanced due diligence

properties.definition: Databases tracking negative news and financial crime indicators
properties.regulatory_significance: Reputational risk and financial crime detection

name: Mandatory AML Screening at Account Opening

properties.rule_statement: AML screening must be performed before or during account opening

name: Ongoing AML Monitoring for High-Risk Customers

properties.rule_statement: Continuous AML screening must be applied to customers classified as high-risk

name: Automatic Account Freeze and SAR Filing on Sanctions Hit

properties.rule_statement: A match on a sanctions list triggers automatic account freeze and Suspicious Activity Report filing

name: Account Application Rejection on Failed Screening

properties.rule_statement: An account application must be rejected if AML screening fails

properties.definition: A report filed with National Crime Agency (NCA) for suspected financial crime.
subtypes: Large cash deposit followed by immediate transfer out
Multiple accounts opened with similar identity
Round-tripping (money in and out same day)
business_rules: SAR must be filed within 30 days of suspicion
Customer not informed (reporting confidential)
Account may be frozen pending investigation
Failure to file SAR = regulatory breach

key_functions: Receives and processes Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs)
Assigns NCA reference numbers to filed reports

properties.rule_statement: SAR must be filed within 30 days of suspicion
Customer must not be informed about the SAR filing
Account may be frozen pending investigation
Failure to file a SAR constitutes a regulatory breach

properties.definition: A system-generated alert when fraud is detected.
business_rules: High-risk alerts automatically block transaction
Customer can dispute fraud
Bank reimburses unauthorized fraud within 10 days
Fraud data feeds ML model for continuous improvement

key_functions: Manual review of high-risk fraud alerts

properties.rule_statement: High-risk alerts automatically block transaction
Customer can dispute fraud
Bank reimburses unauthorized fraud within 10 days
Fraud data feeds ML model for continuous improvement

properties.purpose: Continuous improvement of fraud detection based on historical fraud data

An entity that holds an Account
A container that holds a Customer and records Transactions
A recorded event contained within an Account
A specific variant or subtype of Account
Entity that offers Features and serves as parent for Accounts
Capability offered by a Product, made available within an Account
Container associated with a Product that holds its Features
Specific banking product instance
Product feature enabling joint account functionality
Specific customer account instance linked to a product
Primary entity linked to a Card
Entity linked to an Account that processes Transactions
Event processed by a Card that can trigger a Fraud Alert
Alert generated or triggered by a Transaction
Starting entity in the customer verification and AML screening workflow
Verification step linked to customer identity and compliance checks
Anti-money laundering screening process executed after KYC verification
Report generated when AML screening triggers a suspicious activity alert
Core financial record tracking product linkage, transaction-derived balance, and lifecycle status
business_rules: Mandatory Product Linkage Constraint
Balance Derivation Rule
Dormancy Threshold Rule
Account Status Enumeration Constraint

properties.rule_statement: Account must have product (account.product_id NOT NULL)
properties.enforcement: Database NOT NULL validation on product_id field

properties.rule_statement: Account balance equals the sum of all transactions (account.balance = SUM(transactions))
Account status transitions to Dormant if inactive for over 12 months (account_status = 'Dormant' IF no_activity > 365 days)
properties.enforcement: Automated inactivity monitoring and status update trigger

properties.rule_statement: Account status must be strictly one of the defined enumerated values (account.status IN Active, Dormant, Closed, Suspended)
properties.enforcement: Database enumeration validation to prevent invalid states

properties.rule_statement: Must have identity verified
Must pass AML screening
Must have valid address
properties.technical_expression: customer.address IS NOT NULL AND verified

properties.rule_statement: Age 18+
transaction.amount > 0
transaction.account_id REFERENCES account(account_id)
transaction.status IN (Pending, Completed) AND cannot_edit_completed

properties.definition: Report filed upon detection of suspicious activity
business_rules: Must be filed within 30 days of suspicion date
properties.regulatory_significance: Subject to strict filing deadline compliance constraint

properties.rule_statement: SAR.filing_date <= suspicion_date + 30 days

properties.what_it_measures: Time elapsed between suspicion date and SAR filing
properties.context: Ensures timely regulatory reporting of suspicious activities

properties.definition: Customer identity verification process requiring periodic refresh
business_rules: Must be refreshed every 3 to 5 years
properties.regulatory_significance: Mandatory periodic review for customer due diligence compliance

properties.rule_statement: KYC.next_review_date <= TODAY() + 5 years

properties.what_it_measures: Maximum allowable interval between KYC verification reviews
properties.context: Ensures customer data remains current and compliant with due diligence standards

properties.definition: Process for handling customer fraud claims and processing reimbursements
business_rules: Reimbursement must occur within 10 days of claim date
properties.regulatory_significance: Subject to strict reimbursement deadline compliance

properties.rule_statement: Fraud_claim.reimbursement_date <= claim_date + 10 days

properties.what_it_measures: Maximum allowable time to process fraud claim reimbursement after initial claim
properties.context: Ensures timely customer compensation and regulatory compliance for fraud handling

properties.definition: Root entity representing an individual or organization interacting with the bank
Individual or entity with an active banking relationship
Geographic location associated with a party or customer
Official document used to verify identity and address
business_rules: Must be verified before account activation

properties.definition: Process to verify customer identity and assess risk
business_rules: Identity and address must be verified as TRUE
PEP screening required
properties.regulatory_significance: Mandatory for compliance with anti-money laundering regulations

properties.definition: Process to screen customers against sanctions and watchlists
business_rules: Zero matches required for low risk classification
Screening must pass before account activation
properties.regulatory_significance: Required to prevent financial crime and meet regulatory obligations

properties.definition: Financial container for holding funds and processing transactions
business_rules: Status must be Active to process transactions

properties.definition: Specific savings product variant for customer accounts
Entity legally entitled to operate an account
Record of a financial movement linked to an account
business_rules: Status must be Completed for funds to be recognized

properties.definition: Physical or virtual payment instrument linked to an account
business_rules: Daily limit set at £5,000 for active cards

properties.rule_statement: International standard for financial concepts
Financial data exchange standards
Regulatory requirements
Capital and risk requirements
